input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when the merged dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates at runtime -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code-behind like:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(isDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Activity restarts on Android config change**
Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when the system UI mode changes. In `Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the UI-mode change itself, preventing the restart. You can then listen for it and swap themes:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);
    bool isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR** — `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for runtime updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent the Activity restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user changes the Android system dark mode setting, the app's Activity is being destroyed and recreated. This is default Android behavior for configuration changes. They need to handle this by adding `ConfigChanges` to their `MainActivity`.


I'll walk through both solutions to get their dark mode working properly.
